Gregory W. Sanders
6585 Buckley Drive
Cambria, CA93428
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-04-099
Dear Mr. Sanders:
This letter is in response to your.request for advice on the conflict-oiinterest provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the 'Acf).r The Act requires the Commission to provide written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized
representative. (Section 8311a@); regulation 18329(a), (copy enclosed).) However, the
Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not
in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application oflaw to a givan set of facts provided by the
requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 831 l4;
regulation l8329tb)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide
written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subjict of a
pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, you current question involves
analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already o""rrrrd, such that
issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not frrrther the
impartial, efflective adminiskation of the Act so mandated by section g3l l l.
r' Government Code sections 8 I 000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at title 2,sectiors 1 8 109 -
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
Re
,sez


File No. W-04-099
Page No. 2
Please note that our decision is not a deterrnination of the question posed in your request.
Shoutd you have any questions, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa
C. Scott
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
CSTjg

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for advice on conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act regarding a series of interrelated decisions, some of which had already occurred.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation and the analysis would relate to past conduct rather than future guidance, which would not serve the impartial administration of the Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Declination letter with clear conclusion section. Question section not explicitly labeled but can be inferred from conclusion. Document references pending investigation as basis for declination. OCR quality is generally good with minor artifacts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked for advice on conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act regarding a series of interrelated decisions, some of which had already occurred.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for advice on Political Reform Act conflict-of-interest provisions, citing a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division and the fact that the analysis would relate to past conduct rather than future guidance.
